title: Maldives 10-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Maldives 10-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 10-day trip to Maldives, Maldives: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Overwater villa resort stay and Sunset dolphin cruise. Prices from $4121 per person (estimate).
highlights: Overwater villa with a private infinity pool
Overwater villa stay with glass floor panels
overview: Spend 10 days discovering Maldives, Maldives — Tropical climate that stays warm and humid year-round (highs 30-31°C); May to November brings the monsoon season with more rain and wind, though showers are often brief. December to April (dry season) offers the best combination of sunshine, calm seas and excellent diving visibility; it overlaps with the busiest and most expensive period. This itinerary blends Overwater villa resort stay, Sunset dolphin cruise, Resort house reef snorkeling with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
9 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Overwater villa resort stay, Sunset dolphin cruise, Resort house reef snorkeling
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Maldives
itinerary.description: Land at Velana International Airport (MLE) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Overwater villa resort stay — Signature Maldives accommodation experience over turquoise lagoons. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Maldives: Sunset dolphin cruise & Scuba diving excursion (Manta/whale shark sites)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Sunset dolphin cruise — Boat trip to spot spinner dolphins at dusk. In the afternoon, continue to Scuba diving excursion (Manta/whale shark sites) — World-class reef diving with rays and reef sharks. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Maldives: Snorkeling excursion to a house reef or sandbank & Private sandbank picnic
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Snorkeling excursion to a house reef or sandbank — Guided snorkel trip to nearby coral reefs. In the afternoon, continue to Private sandbank picnic — Exclusive lunch or dinner set up on an isolated sandbank. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Snorkeling excursion to a house reef or sandbank

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Nearby uninhabited sandbank islands
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Nearby uninhabited sandbank islands, a popular side trip from Maldives. Return to Maldives in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Nearby uninhabited sandbank islands

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Local island homestays for authentic culture — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Local island homestays for authentic culture

itinerary.title: Maldives: Seaplane scenic transfer/tour & Submarine or underwater restaurant dining
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Seaplane scenic transfer/tour — Aerial views of atolls en route to a resort. In the afternoon, continue to Submarine or underwater restaurant dining — Dine below the waterline surrounded by marine life. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Local fishing village visits
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Local fishing village visits, a popular side trip from Maldives. Return to Maldives in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Local fishing village visits

itinerary.title: Maldives: Spa treatment (overwater spa pavilion) & Big game fishing charter
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Spa treatment (overwater spa pavilion) — Signature resort spa experience over the lagoon. In the afternoon, continue to Big game fishing charter — Deep-sea fishing trip for tuna and marlin. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Fulhadhoo Island bioluminescent beach — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Local island cultural tour or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to MLE for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book seaplane transfers as early as possible — daylight-only operating hours can create scheduling bottlenecks in peak season.
Confirm whether your resort island allows alcohol and swimwear-only zones since rules differ from local islands.
Pack reef-safe sunscreen; some resorts ban oxybenzone-based sunscreen to protect the coral.
Budget travelers can stay on local islands with guesthouses for a fraction of resort prices, using public ferries.
May-November monsoon season still has plenty of sun — showers are typically short and rates are notably lower.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Reef-safe sunscreen (many resorts require it to protect coral)
Modest cover-up for transiting through Malé or visiting local islands
Snorkel gear if you have your own preferred fit
Light, breathable clothing — it is hot and humid year-round
thingsToDo: Snorkeling excursion to a house reef or sandbank
Scuba diving with manta rays and whale sharks
thingsToAvoid: Bringing alcohol, pork products, or overtly religious materials of other faiths into the country — customs restrictions apply.
Standing on or touching coral reefs while snorkeling — irreversible damage occurs quickly.
Wearing swimwear on local, inhabited islands outside of designated bikini beaches.
Booking a resort without confirming seaplane vs speedboat transfer time/cost — some remote atolls add significant transfer expense.
Assuming all resorts include meals — many operate on room-only or bed-and-breakfast plans with costly à la carte dining.
topRestaurants: Ithaa Undersea Restaurant — Fine Dining Underwater ($$$$)
Symphony (Soneva Fushi) — International Resort Fine Dining ($$$$)
Local teashops in Malé — Maldivian ("hedhikaa" short eats) ($)
Hi Bar & Grill (resort dining) — International Beachfront ($$$)
Sala Boutique & Café, Malé — Café / Light Bites ($$)
Malé fish market food stalls — Maldivian Seafood ($)
Kanuma (Cheval Blanc Randheli) — Japanese Fine Dining ($$$$)
hiddenGems: Local island homestays for authentic culture
familyActivities: Family snorkeling excursions on the house reef
Kids' turtle and dolphin spotting cruise
Resort kids' clubs and marine biology lessons
adventureActivities: Scuba diving with manta rays and whale sharks
luxuryExperiences: Overwater villa with a private infinity pool
Private seaplane charter for a scenic tour
Private sandbank dinner set up by resort staff
honeymoonExperiences: Overwater villa stay with glass floor panels
Couples spa treatment over the lagoon
Candlelit dinner on a private sandbank
budgetExperiences: Stay on a local island guesthouse instead of a resort
Free house-reef snorkeling at budget guesthouses
Public beach time on Hulhumalé or Malé's Artificial Beach
Local island cultural walks and teashop snacks
localEtiquette: Dress modestly on local inhabited islands — swimwear is reserved for resort islands and designated bikini beaches.
Alcohol is only served on resort islands, not on local islands, in line with Islamic law.
Remove shoes before entering mosques, and dress conservatively if visiting one.
Public displays of affection should be minimal, especially on local islands.
Respect marine life — never touch coral or stand on reefs while snorkeling or diving.
localTransportGuide: Getting between islands means either a speedboat transfer (shorter distances) or a scenic seaplane (further atolls), both typically arranged directly through your resort. Within resort islands, transport is by foot or bicycle since most are small enough to cross in 10-15 minutes.
bestTimeToVisit: December to April (dry season) offers the best combination of sunshine, calm seas and excellent diving visibility; it overlaps with the busiest and most expensive period.
weather: Tropical climate that stays warm and humid year-round (highs 30-31°C); May to November brings the monsoon season with more rain and wind, though showers are often brief.
visaInformation: Visa-free entry on arrival for all nationalities for tourist stays (typically up to 30 days), provided travelers have a confirmed hotel/resort booking, sufficient funds and a return ticket. No advance visa application is needed for most visitors.
averageFlightTime: ~10.5h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Maldives?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry on arrival for all nationalities for tourist stays (typically up to 30 days), provided travelers have a confirmed hotel/resort booking, sufficient funds and a return ticket. No advance visa application is needed for most visitors.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Maldives?
faqs.answer: December to April (dry season) offers the best combination of sunshine, calm seas and excellent diving visibility; it overlaps with the busiest and most expensive period. Tropical climate that stays warm and humid year-round (highs 30-31°C); May to November brings the monsoon season with more rain and wind, though showers are often brief.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Maldives safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Maldives is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Maldives?
faqs.answer: The local currency is MVR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Maldives is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Overwater villa stay with glass floor panels experience.
